slides.lead: Demo (sample) presentation of this template. All copy is dummy and serves only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the brand or event name here, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared obsession with skin.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses colored initials instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: The brand, the shelf and the moments that matter most.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Customers no longer buy a single product, they buy a routine they trust morning and night. Value grows when the steps connect.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Great for a key point or a chapter opener.

items: repeat purchase after the routine bundle launched
average rating across the hero serum line
of revenue from returning loyalty members
bullets: Measure loyalty, not just first orders: it shows real love for the product.
Start where the skin concern is clearest and the demand is strongest.
A small bundle with a clear promise beats a wide catalog without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: Four simple steps anyone can keep up.
bullets: Lifts away the day without stripping the skin barrier.
Targets the concern with an active that actually works.
Locks in moisture so results last through the night.
Shields against sun and pollution every morning.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales to fit.

slides.lead: Same skin, two strategies and two very different results.
cards: Mask the problem for a night. Cheap but never lasting.
Buy whatever is viral. Crowded shelf, no real plan.
Consistent steps matched to the skin. Slow but lasting.
Hero actives daily, treats and masks as needed.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a clean beauty line.
cards: Proven ingredients, no needless filler or harsh additives.
Stable textures that feel as good as they perform.
Dermatologist checks before anything reaches a face.
Full ingredient lists customers can actually read.
Refillable formats that cut waste on every reorder.
A team that answers skin questions with real advice.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in the header of each card. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to deepen loyalty and basics.
A hero product catches on and reshapes the whole range.
The brand moves from skincare into full daily rituals.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: The brands that win are not the ones with the most products, but the ones their customers trust on their own skin.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left bar. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget in the rush.
bullets: Every product has a named person who stands behind it.
You know up front how customers will judge success.
On anything risky, safety decides before the trend.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A launch without a promise and an owner ends up forgotten.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the tool and create a personal skin routine live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
